expectations.text: Response creates collection with Zod schema
expectations.evidence: Code defines taskSchema using z.object({...}) with fields id, title, description, status (z.enum), priority, assigneeId, projectId and uses it in createCollection

expectations.text: Response uses createCollection with getKey function
expectations.evidence: Response uses createCollection with 'primaryKey: "id"' instead of a getKey function. The API used is: createCollection<Task>({ name: 'tasks', schema: taskSchema, primaryKey: 'id' }) which does not match the expected getKey pattern

expectations.text: Response implements useLiveQuery for reactive data
expectations.evidence: TaskBoard uses useLiveQuery: const tasks = useLiveQuery(() => taskCollection.query({ where: { projectId }, orderBy: { priority: 'asc' } }))

expectations.text: Response groups tasks by status in the TaskBoard component
expectations.evidence: Tasks are grouped by status using .filter(): todoTasks = tasks.filter(t => t.status === 'todo'), inProgressTasks = tasks.filter(t => t.status === 'in-progress'), doneTasks = tasks.filter(t => t.status === 'done') and rendered in separate columns

expectations.text: Response includes mutation handlers (onInsert, onUpdate, onDelete)
expectations.evidence: The createCollection call does not include onInsert, onUpdate, or onDelete handlers. The response only mentions that 'For server persistence, you would need to integrate a sync engine or backend API calls' in the Notes section
